8 parallel psql SELECT count(*) connections all succeeded (8/8 identical results: 310)

--auth-file (SCRAM-SHA-256, hashed-at-rest): right password accepted (select 1 -> 1); wrong password rejected (psql: error: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5447 failed: FATAL: Password authentication failed for user "" connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5447 failed: FATAL: Password authentication failed for user ""); unknown user rejected (psql: error: connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5447 failed: FATAL: password authentication failed for user "not_a_real_user" connection to server at "127.0.0.1", port 5447 failed: FATAL: password authentication failed for user "not_a_real_user"). Servers without --auth-file stay permissive and log a startup WARN.

--tls-cert/--tls-key (rustls; boot fails hard on bad cert/key, no plaintext fallback): sslmode=require -> 1; openssl s_client -starttls postgres handshake: TLSv1.3, Cipher is TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384; sslmode=verify-full against the pinned dev cert -> 1. Plaintext startup is still accepted on the same listener (upstream/stock-Postgres behavior) — clients enforce encryption via sslmode.
INSERT trip_id=900133 returned tag 'INSERT 0 1'; row read back over a NEW connection: 900133|Parity B1

UPDATE trip_id=900133 returned tag 'UPDATE 1'; new fare read back over a NEW connection: 99.9 (copy-on-write: only the Parquet file holding the row is rewritten, all other files are reused in the new Iceberg snapshot)
DELETE trip_id=900134 returned tag 'DELETE 1'; count over a NEW connection afterwards: 0; time travel intact: pre-delete snapshot 2345083741218778863 still serves the row (count=1)

real transactions: ROLLBACK undid the INSERT (row 900135 absent afterwards; the row WAS visible inside the txn: 'BEGIN INSERT 0 1 count(*) ---------- 1 (1 row) ROLLBACK'); a 2-statement txn (INSERT + UPDATE) COMMITted atomically as one Iceberg snapshot (row 900136|fare=77.0 from a new connection: 'BEGIN INSERT 0 1 UPDATE 1 COMMIT'). Reads inside a txn are snapshot-pinned per table (snapshot isolation, first-committer-wins 40001 on conflict — proven in e2e (j)).

--enforce-pk + table property icegres.primary-key=id: first insert accepted ('INSERT 0 1'); duplicate rejected with 23505 (ERROR: duplicate key value violates unique constraint "parity_pk_pkey": key (id) = (1) is not unique); NULL key rejected with 23502 (ERROR: null value in column(s) (id) of relation "parity_pk" violates not-null constraint (primary key, 1 row(s))); table holds 1 row. Checks run against the snapshot each commit anchors to (409 retry re-validates), so racing duplicates cannot both land. Default is OFF (documented cost: reads key columns of every live file per write).

probes.behavior: data in open format, other engines can read
probes.evidence: independent reader pyiceberg 0.11.1 read demo.trips via the REST catalog + S3: 312 rows == server's count(312)
Lakekeeper GET /v1/563d3f92-7884-11f1-ab2a-cba89bbcc662/namespaces/demo/tables lists: ["cities","trips"]

Moonlink exists to replicate Postgres heap data into Iceberg; icegres data is born in Iceberg (INSERT via wire commits an Iceberg snapshot directly, verified by B1+C1) — there is no second copy to synchronize.

row committed via conn A was readable from a new connection ~64ms after commit (coarse; includes psql startup — precise p50/p95 is bench freshness_ms). Moonlink bar: sub-second. Additionally: buffered mode (--write-buffer-ms 100, opt-in; default 0 = synchronous): INSERT acked from the in-memory buffer was readable from a NEW connection on the same server after ~57ms (union read — no wait for the Iceberg commit; precise p50/p95 is bench insert_single_buffered_ms/freshness_buffered_ms; cross-server freshness = flush cadence, unclean-kill loss window <= 100ms, WARNed at startup).

demo."trips$snapshots" queryable (21 snapshots listed; $manifests also works). Caveats: 'select *' fails on the Map-typed summary column (ERROR: Unsupported Datatype Map("key_value": non-null Struct("key": non-null Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "7"}, "value": Utf8, metadata: {"PARQUET:field_id": "8"}), unsorted)) and count(*) hits a DataFusion logical/physical schema mismatch — column projections work.
killed and restarted the :5440 compute: row count identical before/after (313), wire-inserted row trip_id=900133 intact — all state lives in Iceberg/RustFS, none in the process.

probes.behavior: multiple stateless computes on shared storage
probes.evidence: second 'icegres serve' on :5440 (same catalog, started before the write) returned identical count (313) and served row trip_id=900133 committed via :5439 after both computes started (visible on first poll, 49ms; each query reloads table metadata from the REST catalog, so no staleness window was observed).
serve spawn -> first successful 'select 1': 41ms (single run, 50ms poll; p50/p95 over >=5 runs is bench cold_start_ms). Neon bar: ~500ms-few s.

snapshot-pinned read works: demo."trips@7928055737845185364" (oldest snapshot from "trips$snapshots") returned count=280 vs current count=313 (rows appended this run are invisible in the pinned view; WHERE on the pinned table -> 280). Read-only; timestamp syntax FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF is not supported by datafusion 52.

--idle-shutdown-secs 5: server answered queries (demo.cities count=20), then exited on its own 5047ms after the last connection with code 0 (clean); health pings on :5446 do not reset the idle clock. Scale-from-zero: fresh spawn answered 'select 1' after 41ms (supervisor pattern documented in icegres/src/ops.rs + README).

Neon branch-per-endpoint over Iceberg snapshot refs: 'icegres branch create demo.trips parity_dev' forked main's head with zero data copied; a second server (icegres serve --branch parity_dev on :5450) took INSERT trip_id=99029846 + UPDATE (fare -> 123.0) committed to the branch ref with assert-ref-snapshot-id; main endpoint on :5439 stayed untouched (row count for the id 0 -> 0, total 313 -> 313); 'branch drop' removed only the ref.

serve log contains structured fields catalog_uri/warehouse/s3_endpoint/listen_addr: 2026-07-06T00:51:23.673241Z INFO icegres: connecting to Iceberg REST catalog catalog_uri=http://127.0.0.1:8181/catalog warehouse=lakehouse s3_endpoint=http://127.0.0.1:9000 2026-07-06T00:51:23.694985Z INFO icegres: starting pgwire server listen_addr=127.0.0.1:5440

two probes work: (1) pgwire connect + 'select 1' as the readiness check (what the harnesses use; plain TCP connect works for tcpSocket-style checks); (2) dedicated HTTP liveness endpoint via --health-port — curl http://127.0.0.1:5446/health during the D5 probe returned 200 'ok'.
'icegres serve' booted with ZERO flags — all config from ICEGRES_* env vars (host/port/catalog/warehouse/S3) — and answered on :5444 (demo.cities count=20)
